auth_notes: Requires custom header-based authentication (e.g., Authorization: Bearer <token>) to connect n8n to a Human-in-the-loop (HITL) service or a custom-built review dashboard.
rate_limits: Varies by provider; custom implementations typically allow 10-100 requests per second.
gotchas: n8n 'Wait' nodes have execution timeouts; long-term reviews (days/weeks) require external state management in a database.
Securing the callback webhook is critical to prevent unauthorized approval injections.
Handling race conditions if multiple reviewers attempt to process the same task simultaneously.
client_must_provide: API credentials for the review platform (e.g., Humanloop, Labelbox, or custom DB)
Publicly accessible Webhook URL for decision callbacks
Data schema for the review payload
research_notes: Implementation requires a two-part workflow: 1. An HTTP Request node to send data to the review interface. 2. A Webhook node (or Wait node set to 'On Webhook Call') to pause the workflow until a human submits a decision. State should be tracked via a unique 'External ID' to map the callback to the original execution.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Implementation requires a two-part workflow: 1. An HTTP Request node to send data to the review interface. 2. A Webhook node (or Wait node set to 'On Webhook Call') to pause the workflow until a human submits a decision.
